microsoft_ft: Defined program objectives and execution strategy for Azure's recovery validation program, scaling from a 2-person operation into a **platformized system** sustaining 45+ annual resilience drills and driving **$14M+ business impact** across Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led 0→1 development of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, authoring product requirements and self-service scheduling specs that **reduced operational toil by 30%** and transitioned drill execution to a scalable, repeatable model.

Directed **14 cross-org program executions** under executive visibility, including serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract, ensuring compliance with functional policies and stakeholder alignment.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new continuous-validation methodology.

Built an internal AI agent and restructured planning workflows to **cut planning cycle time by 39%** and increase drill capacity by 21%, disseminating the new process model across partner engineering teams.
microsoft_2023: Championed adoption of AI-driven code generation workflows, conducting user demos and training that drove utilization across **14 key teams** and saved **37 engineering hours monthly**, directly improving resource utilization and program productivity.

Influenced product roadmap to include intent-based YAML generation by facilitating 11+ structured interviews with Azure service teams, documenting critical feature gaps and disseminating findings to senior stakeholders to shift prioritization.

Improved data integrity and retrieval by enforcing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation to an AI-powered semantic search tool, **cutting lookup time by 83%** and enabling faster cross-functional information transfer.
microsoft_2022: Secured cross-functional alignment across **140+ teams** on a unified automation prioritization framework, generating $3M in accelerated revenue and launching regions **28% faster** by ensuring resource utilization was directed to highest-impact paths.

Conducted discovery with 20+ service teams to quantify 81 hours of manual effort per region launch, then built and disseminated a phased roadmap to automate critical paths and reduce program execution risk.

Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to identify automation gaps, reallocate resources effectively, and report program progress against milestones.
amazon_robotics: Defined the legacy OS migration strategy and mapped dependencies across 1,200+ stations, achieving **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by ensuring all preliminary planning and viability activities were completed before execution.

Facilitated Agile sprint planning and retrospectives, prioritizing the backlog to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilize the developer intake process, maximizing team productivity throughout the program lifecycle.

Drove strategic alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement automated CI/CD pipelines, **accelerating software deployment cycles by 25%** and ensuring newly developed processes were disseminated across functional areas.
pro_painters: Managed end-to-end scoping and invoicing lifecycle for **200+ monthly proposals** via a new CRM process, increasing job bookings by 26% and ensuring all project documentation was accurately reported across operational functions.

Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and boosted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing the website and Google profile to improve program-level resource and budget effectiveness.

Conducted financial profitability analysis across **1,000+ project records** to identify margin leaks, delivering data-driven recommendations to leadership that improved pricing models and material resource utilization.

tailoring_notes: JD term 'resource utilization' woven into microsoft_ft bullet 1 (platformized system, resource scale) and microsoft_2022 bullet 1 (framework alignment) and pro_painters bullet 1 (CRM lifecycle).

JD term 'disseminated' (documentation/information transfer requirement) woven into microsoft_ft bullet 5 (process model dissemination), microsoft_2023 bullet 2 (findings to stakeholders), and amazon_robotics bullet 3 (processes disseminated across functional areas).

JD term 'preliminary activities / milestones / viability studies' woven into amazon_robotics bullet 1 explicitly ('preliminary planning and viability activities completed before execution'), mirroring the JD's program setup language.
JD emphasis on 'documentation and compliance' reflected in microsoft_ft bullet 3 (compliance with functional policies) and pro_painters bullet 1 (documentation accurately reported).
Dropped one bullet each from microsoft_2023 and amazon_robotics (to their minimums) and tightened phrasing across all roles to resolve page overflow while keeping every bullet within 250–290 chars.
